text: Working with APIs allows you to connect different software systems. When you make an HTTP request to an API, you typically receive data back in JSON format, which is lightweight and easy to read. Before accessing many APIs, you need authentication, often using an API key, to verify your identity. It's also important to be aware of rate limits, which control how many requests you can make within a certain time. Finally, always implement error handling in your code. APIs can return errors for many reasons, such as invalid input or server issues, so handling them gracefully ensures your application runs smoothly.
